qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Login endpoint accepts credentials via GET query parameters exposing them in server logs and browser history
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding concerns GET credential handling in objects/login.json.php and logging/session-token exposure, not raw videos_id concatenation in objects/like.php::getLike().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated status endpoint exposes PHP session ID, application version, and plugin inventory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding concerns unauthenticated objects/status.json.php information disclosure, not SQL injection through videos_id in objects/like.php::getLike().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection root cause in objects/like.php::getLike(), where videos_id is concatenated into the likes query while users_id is parameterized.
evidence: The advisory identifies objects/like.php::getLike() and the query SELECT * FROM likes WHERE users_id = ? AND videos_id = $this->videos_id LIMIT 1 as the vulnerable code/root cause.

credential-exposure-get-login reports GET-based credential handling in objects/login.json.php and related logging/session-token exposure; it does not report the likes SQL query, getLike(), or a videos_id SQL injection sink.
session-id-exposure-status reports unauthenticated information disclosure in objects/status.json.php; it does not report the likes SQL query, getLike(), or a videos_id SQL injection sink.
The session execution history notes that videos_id SQL injection in like.php was investigated but was not promoted to a final qualifying finding.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
